problem: Focused audio cue sweeps can find local candidates that look promising in isolation, then fail once placed in the full Aurora theme and event-gap scoring context.
strategy: Replace one cue in the latest full-theme metrics with a representative candidate capture, run the normal event-gap scorer against that synthetic theme, and block runtime promotion unless the cue and aggregate guardrails improve.
successMeasure: A candidate may be promoted only when focused keeper gates, full-theme cue gap, worst-segment risk, theme average risk, and semantic score all hold or improve.

wins: Theme average worst-segment risk improves by 0.27/10.

currentCueRow.label: Ship Loss
currentCueRow.focus: Compare whether ship loss feels like a real Galaga death moment rather than a generic impact.
currentCueRow.worstSegmentInterpretation: body reference sub-event is not separated in runtime; the cue is collapsing multiple reference moments into one continuous segment.
currentCueRow.referenceSegmentationResolution: relative to full galaga3-death clip; labels preserve long death onset, sustained body, and final tail
currentCueRow.referenceSegmentationSource: curated from active-window reads in aurora-audio-theme-comparison and repeated playerHit candidate pass
currentCueRow.playerMeaning: The player lost a ship; the cue should be unmistakable and synchronized with death feedback.
currentCueRow.semanticRecommendation: Keep semantic mapping as a guardrail while acoustic/timing gaps are tuned.
currentCueRow.recommendation: Tune the body segment first: body reference sub-event is not separated in runtime; the cue is collapsing multiple reference moments into one continuous segment.

nextStep: Try playerHit in the Aurora audio theme only as a measured runtime trial, then accept it only if full audio comparison, event gap, cue alignment, and quality scoring all hold or improve.
